description: In an apartment living room, Chandler sits in a black recliner reading a paperback book.
A loud off-screen thud occurs. Chandler flinches backward slightly and looks around with wide eyes.
Chandler closes his book and pushes himself up from the recliner.
Chandler hurries toward an open doorway and stops, looking out into the hall.
Standing in the doorway, Chandler gestures outward with his right hand and says, "See, Joe, that's why your parents told you not to jump on the bed."
The scene fades to black. Audience laughter is audible.
The screen remains black. Instrumental transition music begins to play.

The setting changes to an exterior view of a multi-story city building. The scene then shifts to an apartment kitchen, where Monica stands behind an island counter covered with boxes of fruit. Ross and Rachel walk through the front door.
Monica smiles, holding up a wooden spoon, and says, "Hey, look at me. I'm making jam."
Ross and Rachel walk toward the counter.
Monica continues, "04:00 this morning."
Rachel stands near the counter with her hands on her hips.
Rachel asks, "Where'd you get fruit at four in the morning?"
Monica lifts a flat box of fruit and says, "Went down to the docks. Bet you didn't know you can get it wholesale. I"
Rachel lowers her arms and replies, "didn't know there were docks."
The apartment door opens. Joey walks in wearing an arm sling over his left arm. Chandler follows him inside.
Ross points at Joey and says, "Hey. Oh, is it broken?"
Joey stops, touches his sling with his right hand, and says, "No, I got to wear this thing for a couple of weeks."
Rachel gestures toward Joey and asks, "Did you tell the doctor you did it jumping up and down"
Rachel finishes her question, saying, "on your". Joey shakes his head and answers, "no. I had a whole story worked out, but then Chandler sold me out."
Chandler, standing behind Joey, gestures slightly and says, "Well, I'm sorry,"
Chandler continues, "Joe. I didn't think the doctor was going to buy that. It just fell out of the socket." Joey looks at him with lowered eyebrows.
Joey looks away from Chandler, spots a piece of fruit on the table, and picks it up with his right hand.
Joey holds the fruit up and asks, "What is this?" Ross, standing nearby, replies, "Fruit."
Joey examines the fruit. Monica turns back toward the counter where she was working.
Chandler says, "Monica's making jam." Joey smiles and says, "Whoa, jam. I love jam. Hey, how come we never have jam in our place?"
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler says, "Because the kids need new shoes," keeping his hands in his pockets.
Ross and Rachel look at Monica, who is peeling a piece of fruit.
Monica holds the fruit up and says, "One end of business, people. I'm sick and tired of being depressed about Richard."
Monica continues, "I needed a plan."
Monica says, "Plan to get over my man."
Monica asks, "What's the opposite of man?" gesturing with the fruit in her hand.
Monica answers, "Jam."
Joey walks past Ross and Rachel, heading toward a large pot on the stove. He dips a long spoon into the pot.
Monica notices Joey, reaches toward him, and shouts, "Oh, Joey, don't. It's way too hot." Joey puts the spoon in his mouth, immediately groans, and quickly pulls it out.
Joey turns away from the stove, exhaling heavily and blowing air from his mouth. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey continues blowing air from his mouth, looking downward as he steps back toward the pot.
Joey places the spoon back into the pot and says, "This will just be my batch."
The setting changes to a daytime exterior view of a city skyline. Instrumental transition music plays.
The setting changes to the exterior of a flower shop. Malcolm stands near the floral display. Phoebe walks out of the shop and past him.
Phoebe continues walking down the sidewalk. Malcolm turns to watch her, then begins walking in the same direction, staying several paces behind.
Phoebe stops walking and turns around. Malcolm stops abruptly as she turns.
Phoebe walks back toward Malcolm, raising her hands.
Phoebe stops in front of Malcolm and says, "Guy."
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "Yeah? Why have you been following me all week long? Everywhere I look, there's you."
Malcolm replies, "You wouldn't return my calls. You sent back my"
Malcolm says, "letters." Phoebe asks, "What?" Malcolm replies, "One more chance, Ursula, please," his volume rising slightly on the final words.
Phoebe's expression changes, her mouth opening slightly as her gaze shifts.
Phoebe looks at Malcolm and says, "Well, this is,"
Phoebe maintains her gaze on Malcolm.
Phoebe continues, "um yeah, you want Ursula, and I'm Phoebe twin sisters," gesturing with her hand.
Malcolm stares at Phoebe, his mouth slightly open.
Malcolm asks, "Seriously?"
Phoebe nods.
Malcolm raises his hand to his forehead, rubbing it, and says, "Oh, that's great."
Malcolm lowers his hand and says, "I'm stalking the wrong woman."
Malcolm bows his head forward, letting his arms drop, and says, "I am such a dingus."
Phoebe steps closer to him.
Phoebe says, "You're not a dingus."
Malcolm lowers himself to sit on the stoop stairs.
Sitting on the steps, Malcolm looks up at Phoebe and says, "I want you to know I didn't used to be like this."
Malcolm gestures with his hands and continues, "Before I met your sister, I was like this normal guy who sold beepers and"
Malcolm finishes, "cellular phones."
Phoebe looks down at Malcolm.
Phoebe sits down next to Malcolm and says, "Look, it's not your fault."
Phoebe looks forward.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "This is what she does to guys. Okay,"
Malcolm looks toward the ground, his shoulders slumped.
Malcolm looks at Phoebe and says, "thanks." Phoebe responds, "Wait. You know what? I got a little story."
Phoebe adjusts her posture, turning slightly more toward Malcolm.
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "When I was in junior high school, I went through this period where I thought I was a witch."
Phoebe continues, "And there was this guidance counselor"
Phoebe gestures again and says, "who said something to me that I think will help you a lot."
Phoebe points her finger for emphasis and says, "He said, okay, you're not a witch. You're just an average student."
Malcolm looks at Phoebe, his expression remaining still.
Phoebe asks, "See what I'm saying?"
Malcolm replies, "Not really."
Phoebe keeps her gaze on Malcolm.
In an outdoor setting, Phoebe and Malcolm sit on the concrete steps of a brick building. Phoebe speaks to Malcolm, who looks down. "Well, get over it."
Phoebe gestures with her hands. Malcolm raises his head and nods slightly.
Phoebe continues gesturing as she says, "You seem like a really nice guy."
Phoebe makes a sweeping motion with one arm.
Phoebe says, "Don't be so hard on yourself, okay?" She then stands up.
Malcolm stands, holding a small object in one hand. "You're right. I know you're right. And thanks for being so nice," he replies, making a slight gesture with his free hand.
Malcolm reaches one hand behind his back.
Malcolm brings out a small bouquet of flowers and holds it out toward Phoebe. "Here."
Phoebe smiles and takes the flowers. "Thanks a lot."
Phoebe looks down at the flowers.
Holding the flowers, Phoebe asks, "Do you want to get a cup of coffee?" Malcolm nods, "Yeah."
"Okay," Phoebe says. "Okay," Malcolm agrees. Phoebe turns and begins descending the steps.
Phoebe and Malcolm walk side-by-side along the sidewalk, passing an open storefront where several people stand outside.
Phoebe turns her head toward Malcolm as they walk. "You don't have to walk behind me anymore."
The setting changes to the interior of an apartment. Numerous jars of jam cover the kitchen counters and a small table. The front door opens, and Rachel enters, followed by Ross.
Rachel walks into the kitchen area, surveying the jars. "Mon. Mon?" she calls out.
Rachel reaches out and picks up a small piece of paper resting among the jars.
Reading aloud from the paper, Rachel says, "Gone for more jars. Back later. Monica Geller." She places the paper back down. Ross stands behind her.
Ross steps forward.
Ross places his hands on his hips and looks around the room. "Wait a minute. Look."
Rachel turns to face Ross. "What?" Ross gestures toward the room with both hands, "Look. Look."
Ross reaches his hands out to Rachel.
Ross takes Rachel's hands. "It's an empty apartment."
Ross pulls Rachel closer, resting his hands on her upper arms. "We're all alone in an empty apartment."
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel wraps her arms around Ross's neck. "Honey, come on. I have to be at work in like, ten minutes and"
Rachel tilts her head back.
Rachel brings her head forward and says, "all right, well, it's not like I'm employee of the year or anything." She embraces Ross, and they walk out of the frame toward the living room.
Ross and Rachel fall backward onto the living room sofa, kissing.
The apartment's front door opens, and Chandler walks in. Ross and Rachel quickly sit upright on the sofa.
Rachel rapidly pulls a large pillow onto her lap. "There it is. Oh, that's what you're talking" she says. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler looks at them from the kitchen area.
Chandler stops near the kitchen counter. "hey," he says.
Rachel smooths her hair with her hands. Chandler remains standing by the kitchen.
Chandler points to his chest. "do I look fat?"
Ross and Rachel look at Chandler.
Ross and Rachel say simultaneously, "No."
Chandler places a hand on his chest. "Okay, I accept that."
Chandler gestures with his hands. "When Janice asked me and I said no, she took that to mean that I was calling her a cow."
Rachel leans forward slightly.
Rachel gestures toward Chandler with her hands. "Okay, walk us through it, honey. Walk us through it."
Chandler steps forward, motioning with his hands. "Okay. Well, Janice said hi. Do I look fat"
"today? And I looked at her" Chandler says, before Ross interrupts, holding up a flat hand. "whoa, whoa, whoa."
Ross stares at Chandler. "You looked at her?"
Ross points a finger at Chandler. Rachel smiles slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross speaks firmly, keeping his finger pointed. "You never look."
Chandler remains still, looking at Ross.
Ross lowers his hand and gestures for emphasis. "You just answer. It's like a reflex. Do I look fat?"
"No," Ross answers quickly.
Chandler listens with his brow furrowed.
"Is she prettier than I am?" Ross asks, immediately replying, "No."
Rachel rests her fingers against her forehead.
Ross states, "Does size matter? No, and it works both ways." Rachel nods.
Chandler walks over and sits on a chair positioned across from the sofa.
Chandler gestures toward Ross and Rachel. "Okay, so you both just know this stuff."
Rachel replies, gesturing with both hands, "After about 30 or 40 fights, you kind of catch on."
Ross leans forward.
Ross points a finger at Chandler. "Okay, for instance, let's say Janice is coming back from a trip and she gives you two options."

Ross holds up fingers to count the options. "Option number one she'll take a cab home from the airport. Or option number two you can meet her at baggage claim. What do you do?" Chandler interrupts, "That's"
"easy. Baggage claim" Chandler answers confidently. Ross makes a loud buzzing sound. "Ehnt. Wrong."
Ross points at Chandler. "Now you're single."
Chandler's eyes widen slightly. Audience laughter plays.
Ross holds up three fingers. "It's actually secret option number three you meet her at the gate. That way she knows you love her." Rachel looks at Ross and smiles.
Chandler leans forward toward Ross.
Chandler holds up an index finger. "Okay, this is good. All right, listen. I have one."
Ross and Rachel look at Chandler.
Chandler speaks, moving his hands as he explains. "Janice likes to cuddle at night, which I'm all for. But when you want to go to sleep, you"
Chandler pulls his hands apart. "want some space."
"So how do I tell her that, you know? Accidentally calling her fat, or" Chandler says.
Rachel interrupts, wrapping one arm around Ross's shoulders, "oh, honey,"
Rachel kisses Ross on the cheek. "I'm sorry. We can't help you there because we're cuddly sleepers."
Chandler shudders, scrunching his facial features and exposing his teeth.
Rachel stands up, picking up her bag. "Okay, I'm late for work. All right. Are you guys going to come?" Ross remains seated, "Yeah, yeah. I'm right behind you."
Rachel walks toward the door. "Okay. Good luck, Chandler." Chandler replies, "Thank you, Rachel." She opens the door and exits, saying, "Bye, honey,"
Ross watches the door close behind Rachel, then stands up.
Ross points a finger at Chandler. "Okay, the sleeping thing?"
Ross walks toward Chandler.
Ross stops near Chandler. "Very tricky business, but there is something you can do."
Chandler looks up at Ross. "I thought you guys were cuddly."
Ross shakes his head side to side. "Sleepy? No,"
Ross turns his head to look back at the closed front door.
Ross lowers his volume, bending down toward Chandler. "not cuddly. Not me. Just her. I'm like you. I need the room. Okay, come here." He motions with his hand for Chandler to move closer.
Chandler leans his head closer to Ross as the segment ends.
In an apartment living room, Chandler and Ross sit side-by-side on a sofa.
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "Okay, you're in bed."
Chandler replies, "Yeah."
Ross stands up, reaches over Chandler, and grabs a large cushion from the sofa.
Holding the cushion, Ross says, "I'm going to use the cushion."
"Yeah," Chandler replies, as Ross sits back down beside him.
Ross wraps his arms around the cushion, pressing it against his chest. "Okay, you're in bed. She's over on your side, cuddling. Now, you wait for her to drift"
"off, and then you hug her and roll her over to her side of the" Ross holds the cushion tightly and rolls his upper body over Chandler's legs, moving to the other side of Chandler on the sofa.
"bed. And then you" Ross sits upright on Chandler's left side.
Ross gestures toward the cushion with his hand.
"roll away," Ross says, pushing the cushion gently back toward Chandler.
Ross rests his head on Chandler's shoulder. Chandler looks down at him.
Ross says softly, "Hug for her."
Ross quickly rolls his upper body away, leaning against the opposite armrest.
Ross says, "Roll for you."
Ross sits back up, pulling the cushion between them.
Chandler gestures to the cushion and says, "Okay. The old hug and roll?" Ross replies, "Yeah." Chandler points a finger forward, "Okay, one question." Ross says, "Shoot."
"You're pretending the pillow is a girl, right?" Chandler asks, his gaze steady on Ross.

Ross stares back at Chandler, his expression still. The setting changes to a coffee shop. Joey sits at a small table, his left arm in a dark sling, eating from a plate next to a jar of jam. Rachel, wearing an apron, stands beside his table.
Joey says, "Remember when you were a kid and your mom had dropped you off at the movies with a jar of jam and a little spoon?"
Rachel stares at him, her lips slightly parted. Joey scoops another spoonful of jam into his mouth.
Rachel says, "You're so pretty."
Joey tilts his head and looks up as Rachel turns and walks away. Chandler, Ross, and Rachel are visible seated on a couch in the background.
Phoebe walks into the coffee shop and approaches the group.
Phoebe waves and says, "Hi." Overlapping voices respond with greetings.
"Hey. Oh, you know that guy who's been following me? I talked to him today," Phoebe says, stopping near Joey's table.
Joey asks, "You talked to him?" speaking with food in his mouth and spitting slightly. Phoebe shuts her eyes briefly, then brushes off her chest.
Phoebe says, "Okay. First, I'm not crazy. And second, say it, don't spray it."
Phoebe continues, "Anyway. His name is Malcolm."
"And he wasn't following me."
"I mean, he was, but just because he thought I was Ursula."
Phoebe raises her hands and gestures outward. "And"
"that's why he couldn't just come up and talk to me because of the restraining order."
Joey stares at her, holding his spoon motionless. Chandler turns his head toward Phoebe from the couch.
Chandler says, "Not feeling better about Malcolm?"
Phoebe turns toward Chandler on the couch.
"No," Phoebe says. "He's not like a kook. No, he's just this very passionate, incredibly romantic guy that just got, like, a teensy bit carried"
"away. And we just get along really well. And he is so cute," she adds, her lips curling up into a smile.
Ross looks at Rachel, who looks back at him.
Ross says, "Oh, my God. You've got a crush on your sister's stalker."
Phoebe gestures with her hand. "No, it's going to help him get de-Ursula-ized like I did for Joey. After he went out with her,"
Joey raises his spoon, pointing it toward Phoebe.
Joey says, "I asked for the news, not the weather."
Phoebe looks back at Joey. Monica enters the coffee shop carrying a jar.
Monica says, "Hey, guys." Overlapping greetings are heard from the group.
Monica walks over to Joey's table.
Monica places the jar in front of him. "Joey, this is for you. Blackberry currant."
Joey pulls his spoon from his mouth, picks up the jar, and scoops out a spoonful to eat.
Joey tilts his head back, closing his eyes and exhaling audibly. Monica smiles and walks away.
Chandler leans forward from the couch and says, "Okay, Joe, I gotta ask. The girl from the Xerox place, buck naked, or a big"
"tub of jam?" Chandler finishes.
Joey looks down, his jaw moving as he chews.
Joey raises both hands, pressing his palms and fingers together. "Put your hands together."
Chandler pulls his head back and raises one hand flat in a stopping gesture, his lips pressed together. Joey goes back to eating.
Monica, standing near the background counter, turns her head back. "Joey, take your time with that. That's my last batch."
"No more jam," she adds.
Joey pauses with his spoon halfway to his mouth, his eyes widening.
In the coffee shop, Joey sits on the couch holding a jar and a spoon.
Joey asks, "Well, what happened to your jam plan?" Monica walks over, sits in an armchair, and answers, "I figured out I'd need to charge $17 a jar just to break even."
Monica smiles and says, "So I've got a new plan now. Babies." Audience laughter plays.
Chandler sits next to Joey on the couch, looking toward Monica.
Chandler says, "Well, you're going to need much bigger jars." Audience laughter follows.
Rachel sits beside Ross on another couch. She rests her hand on Monica's shoulder.
Rachel asks, "What are you talking about?"
Turning to Rachel, Monica says, "I'm talking about me having a baby." Rachel leans forward and asks, "Are you serious?"
Monica gestures with both hands. "Yeah. The great thing about the jam plan was I was taking control of my life."
Monica touches her chest, saying, "So I asked myself, what is the most important"
Monica finishes, "thing to me in the world?"
Smiling, Monica says, "And that's when I came up with the baby plan." Holding a plate with cake, Ross asks, "Well, aren't you forgetting something?"
Ross gestures toward Monica with a fork.
Ross asks, "What is that guy's name? Dad." Audience laughter plays.
Monica shakes her head slightly and looks away.
Monica says, "It took me 28 years to find one man that I want to spend my life with."
Raising her hands outward, she says, "If I have to wait another 28 years, then I'll be 56 before I can"
Monica adds, "have a baby. And that's just stupid."
Phoebe, sitting on the couch's backrest, lowers a yellow mug. Chandler looks toward Monica.
Chandler asks, "That's what's stupid?" Audience laughter is audible.
Monica smiles briefly and clasps her hands in her lap.
Raising her hands with open palms, Monica says, "I don't need an actual man, just a couple of his best swimmers." Audience laughter begins.
Monica leans forward and picks up a green mug from the table.
Holding the mug, Monica says, "And there are places that you can go to get that stuff." Chandler responds, "Down to the docks again." Audience laughter plays.
Monica holds the mug near her mouth as audience laughter continues.
The setting changes to a dark bedroom. A framed picture hangs on the wall. Below it, Chandler lies in bed, his right arm wrapped around Janice, who is facing away from him.
Janice nestles her head against Chandler's shoulder.
Janice says, "Night night. Bing a Ling."
Chandler stares straight upward, his eyes open.
Chandler says, "Night,"
Chandler keeps his gaze fixed toward the ceiling.
Chandler says, "Janice."
Chandler slowly slides his right arm out from under Janice's neck.

Chandler abruptly rolls to his right side, pulling away from Janice. Janice wakes up with a brief vocalization and falls off her side of the bed. Chandler looks back over his shoulder toward her as audience laughter plays.
